expectations.text: Identifies the composer's workaround behavior as the anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: 'treating no synths as a problem to solve instead of a creative direction to explore — which is the exact anti-pattern that produces genre-typical results.'

expectations.text: Names or applies Constraint-Driven Composition — constraint as forcing function
expectations.evidence: 'it's a forcing function... Constraints push you off the genre default, and the genre default is where generic lives.' Cites DOOM no-guitars.

expectations.text: Explains generic sound is a direct consequence of bypassing the constraint
expectations.evidence: 'The generic sound you're hearing is the sound of that constraint being dodged, not the sound of the constraint being too tight.'

expectations.text: Recommends exploring the constraint before adding the restricted element back
expectations.evidence: 'build the entire foundation organic-only first; if a synth ever earns its way in later, it enters morphed with a game asset.'

expectations.text: Does not recommend relaxing or removing the constraint
expectations.evidence: 'stop treating no synths as something to endure until it gets lifted. It won't get lifted, and it's the best thing in the brief.'

expectations.text: Identifies the composer's workaround behavior as the anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: 'it's generic because the composer keeps undermining it... The pad is the sound of not making a decision.'

expectations.text: Names or applies Constraint-Driven Composition — constraint as forcing function
expectations.evidence: 'Strip it out and commit, and the workarounds he's forced to invent become the score's fingerprint.' Frames constraint as differentiation bet.

expectations.text: Explains generic sound is a direct consequence of bypassing the constraint
expectations.evidence: 'Sounds fine but generic is the exact signature of a half-honored constraint.'

expectations.text: Recommends exploring the constraint before adding the restricted element back
expectations.evidence: 'Give it one committed sprint of real organic problem-solving first' before considering any exception.

expectations.text: Does not recommend relaxing or removing the constraint
expectations.evidence: 'commit to constraints... The right time to question no synths is after a genuine, full-hearted exploration' — commit-first, not relax.

expectations.text: Identifies a pitched-tone clash with the music's key, not just a mixing/EQ problem
expectations.evidence: Identifies dissonance as a pitch clash (per-shot transient's dominant pitch landing off-key) and even corrects the premise: 8Hz repetition is below pitch perception, so it's the transient pitch, not fire-rate drone.

expectations.text: Names or applies Tonal Integration
expectations.evidence: 'a Tonal Integration failure, and the governing principle is blunt: it cannot be fixed in the mix.'

expectations.text: States EQ and dynamic ducking cannot fix tonal integration in post-processing
expectations.evidence: Quotes the principle: 'you're fixing a problem that shouldn't be there in the first place'; 'EQ does nothing for dissonance — you cannot equalize a pitch into the right key.'

expectations.text: Recommends tuning the crossbow SFX to the music's key or writing music around the SFX
expectations.evidence: 'pitch-shift the asset so that pitch sits on the root, fifth, or octave' or 'let the SFX constraint dictate the music.'

expectations.text: Does not endorse the EQ or low-cut approach as the correct solution
expectations.evidence: 'put down the mix tools. The composer's low-cut is aimed at the wrong track, and the sound designer's EQ can't touch pitch.'

expectations.text: Identifies a pitched-tone clash with the music's key, not just a mixing/EQ problem
expectations.evidence: 'dissonant = a pitch clash... comb-filters into a buzzy, tonal artifact, and the bolt's metallic ring adds a resonant pitch.'

expectations.text: Names or applies Tonal Integration
expectations.evidence: Frames the root cause as periodicity/comb-filter ('machine-gun effect') and leads with 'de-tonalize'; tuning to root/fifth appears only as a secondary 'if the key is stable' option, not the Tonal Integration principle.

expectations.text: States EQ and dynamic ducking cannot fix tonal integration in post-processing
expectations.evidence: Opposite: recommends 'frequency-targeted sidechain ducking / dynamic EQ' as step 3 of the fix and calls EQ 'necessary.'

expectations.text: Recommends tuning the crossbow SFX to the music's key or writing music around the SFX
expectations.evidence: 'tune the crossbow's dominant tone to the root or fifth of that key' (offered as the stable-key alternative).

expectations.text: Does not endorse the EQ or low-cut approach as the correct solution
expectations.evidence: Endorses the sound designer's EQ ('good for the mud... necessary') and dynamic ducking as part of the correct fix, contra the skill's design-it-in stance.

expectations.text: Names or applies the Reactive Audio principle: match system complexity to gameplay complexity
expectations.evidence: 'the textbook Reactive Audio anti-pattern... The governing principle is match system complexity to gameplay complexity.'

expectations.text: Identifies linear room-by-room combat as the structure where continuous driving music wins
expectations.evidence: 'That's the DOOM (2016) structure exactly... Linear combat wants music that drives continuously, like hitting play on an album.'

expectations.text: Frames the diagnostic as mapping gameplay structure first
expectations.evidence: Runs each proposed input (enemy count, combo, health) through the emotional-context test and maps the transitions that matter.

expectations.text: Warns complexity that interrupts the groove is worse than simplicity that sustains it
expectations.evidence: Verbatim: 'complexity that interrupts the groove is worse than simplicity that sustains it.'

expectations.text: Does not endorse the 8-stem system without qualification
expectations.evidence: 'two of the three proposed drivers are continuous variables that will actively hurt the groove'; recommends prototyping the simple version and A/B testing.

expectations.text: Names or applies the Reactive Audio principle: match system complexity to gameplay complexity
expectations.evidence: 'Count perceptible states, not stems'; adaptive music earns its cost only when states map to something the player cares about.

expectations.text: Identifies linear room-by-room combat as the structure where continuous driving music wins
expectations.evidence: 'A linear crawler is the textbook case for horizontal re-sequencing, not deep vertical layering... you don't need continuous 8-layer response when the game itself is discrete.'

expectations.text: Frames the diagnostic as mapping gameplay structure first
expectations.evidence: 'Enumerate the state combinations the spec produces... list every state the system can emit' before judging the system.

expectations.text: Warns complexity that interrupts the groove is worse than simplicity that sustains it
expectations.evidence: Argues from perceptibility and production cost; does not articulate that mid-combat layer churn interrupts the groove — the specific principle is absent.

expectations.text: Does not endorse the 8-stem system without qualification
expectations.evidence: 'Build the 3-4 state crossfade version first... Don't commit the composer to 8 interlocking stems.'

notes: Discriminator: Tonal Integration (S2) is the strongest — baseline diagnoses periodicity/comb-filter and endorses dynamic EQ/ducking, missing the skill's 'cannot be fixed in post' principle. Reactive Audio (S3) moderately discriminating on the groove-interruption principle.
constraint-driven-composition (S1) is NON-discriminating (both 1.0): the model independently reaches 'commit to the constraint, the workaround is why it sounds generic' without the skill.
